patches.find: it considers direction evaluable enough to compute but not evaluable enough to count. The issue is not
patches.replace: it considers direction evaluable enough to compute but not evaluable enough to count. The field is not incidental logging — it is one of two inputs to the auto_verdict formula (pass = direction_correct AND snr_sufficient). The dome’s scoring code treats it as verdict-relevant, then discards it when it returns the wrong answer. The issue is not
patches.rationale: Closes the 'good engineering' escape hatch: the dome could argue they log direction_correct as complete data practice, not as a reliability claim. This sentence shows direction_correct is a VERDICT INPUT, not diagnostic logging — the dome's own architecture treats it as meaningful, then ignores it when it gives the wrong answer.

patches.find: predictions systematically placed below the noise floor
patches.replace: predictions placed below the noise floor
patches.rationale: With only n=1 documented below-noise prediction (W001), 'systematically' overstates the pattern. A dome defender would rightly say one example doesn't make a system. The structural argument holds at n=1: even a single below-noise prediction is unfalsifiable by construction.

patches.find: is five times smaller than the instruments
patches.replace: is roughly five-and-a-half times smaller than the instruments
patches.rationale: The actual ratio is 4.4/0.80=5.5x. 'Five times' is slightly imprecise. We criticize the dome for sloppy arithmetic so we should be exact.

patches.find: We identify at least seven such parameters
patches.replace: We identify at least eight such parameters
patches.rationale: The TLDR count is inconsistent with the numbered list: (1-2) B(r), (3) kappa, (4) damping, (5) n(r), (6) r_eq, (7-8) NMP constants = 8 parameters. 'Seven' understates by one.

patches.find: We identify at least seven parameters that are fitted to observations
patches.replace: We identify at least eight parameters that are fitted to observations
patches.rationale: Summary count matches TLDR fix. Numbered list has 8 items, both counts should say 'eight'.

patches.find: (5) Finsler scaling function n(r), (6) equatorial ring radius r<sub>eq</sub> = 14,105 km,
patches.replace: (5) Finsler scaling function n(r) (see WIN-065), (6) equatorial ring radius r<sub>eq</sub> = 14,105 km (see WIN-057),
patches.rationale: n(r) and r_eq are listed as fitted parameters in the summary but not discussed in the body paragraphs. Adding cross-references to the WIN detail panels gives readers a path to the supporting evidence without requiring new prose here.

patches.find: "Fitted then locked" is not the same as "derived then tested."
patches.replace: "Fitted then locked" is not the same as "derived then tested." To see the distinction: Newton’s gravitational constant G was measured via Cavendish’s torsion balance — an experiment entirely independent of planetary orbits — then tested against orbital mechanics as a separate prediction. The dome’s κ = 10.9/6.5 nT/μGal was extracted from the same eclipse-magnetic observation it now monitors: the constant IS the observation expressed as a ratio. No independent experiment constrains κ before it is applied to eclipse data.
patches.rationale: A defender could argue 'no model meets that standard — Newton's G was also measured from data.' Adding the Newton/Cavendish contrast shows there ARE legitimately derived constants (independent experimental path), and the dome's kappa is not one of them — it is circular by construction.
